text: Arabic language, broadly defined, has a distinctive and intricate linguistic system for its written, spoken and arbitary forms, therefore, the science of morphology which is called Sharaf in Arabic is one of the corner stones in linguistics of Arabic language and consequently in Arabic grammar. This study makes use of a library research as aims at identifying the linguistic and morphological features that are presented in Surah An-Naba’ that belongs to the 30th Juz of the Holy Qur’an, and identifying all the occurrences of *fi’il mudhari* in the present tense in Surah An-Naba’, the readings, the *dhomir* (pronouns) that are attached to each of these verbs and presenting these forms by applying the rules of *nahwu* and *sharaf* and as such by giving a full grammatical explanation of these verbs. The study discovered that there are 16 *fi’il mudhari* in Surah An-Naba’ which have been identified according to the* wazans* (morphological patterns) to which they belong, and the* dhomir* (pronouns) that are attached to each of them. These discoveries are consequently of great utility for the students and researchers in the field of linguistics of the Holy Qur’an, Arabic language, as well as for those who study the grammar of the Arabic language.
